description_long: This object is a detailed airplane cockpit instrument panel, featuring an array of analog gauges, dials, and switches arranged in a curved metal frame. The front face is densely populated with round instruments, each with distinct markings and needles, likely for monitoring altitude, speed, engine performance, and other flight parameters. The panel includes several toggle switches, rotary knobs, and push buttons, all set into a dark, metallic surface. The backside reveals a complex network of wiring, connectors, and mechanical linkages, indicating the functional depth of the instrumentation. The structure is primarily metallic, with some plastic and glass components visible in the instrument faces and switch covers. The overall appearance suggests this is a vintage or classic aircraft dashboard, possibly from a small propeller-driven airplane.
description: A vintage airplane cockpit instrument panel with numerous analog gauges, switches, and dials set in a curved metal frame. The front is densely packed with instruments, while the rear shows intricate wiring and mechanical components.
description_view_0: The rear of the instrument panel, showing exposed wiring, connectors, and mechanical linkages behind the gauges.
description_view_1: The front face of the panel, displaying a full array of analog gauges, dials, and switches in a curved arrangement.
description_view_2: A slightly angled view of the front, highlighting the depth of the gauges and the arrangement of switches and dials.
description_view_3: The back and underside of the panel, revealing more of the internal wiring and mechanical assemblies.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Not visible; only the cockpit instrument panel is present.
attribute_engine_type: Not visible; only the cockpit instrument panel is present.
attribute_tail_design: Not visible; only the cockpit instrument panel is present.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Not visible; only the cockpit instrument panel is present.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single-seat instrument panel layout.
